methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
CORE POWER, a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Mikal Bøe, has raised $100M+ to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector comprises nine comparable companies (one public, eight private), including peers such as Redwood Materials, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Helion. However, a narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with aligned valuation tiers—only one same-stage peer and one adjacent-stage peer were identified, below the three-datapoint threshold required for reliable median derivation.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology was applied as the primary valuation lever. Using the $100M total raised as a baseline value proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, this method implies a valuation of $120M. This single-method triangulation establishes a narrow range: a floor of $102M and a ceiling of $138M, with a median estimate of $120M.

The valuation range of $102M–$138M reflects meaningful uncertainty. The reliance on a single applicable method, combined with the scarcity of closely comparable Growth-stage peers in the Climate & Energy universe, constrains confidence. Additional data on recent funding round pricing or sector-specific revenue or margin multiples would materially strengthen the analysis. The median of $120M should be treated as illustrative pending further comparable data.
